step_rationale: Platelet membrane protein; thrombin-sensitive; released upon activation.
mechanism: GPV is a thrombin-activated platelet membrane protein released during platelet activation, indicating both thrombin generation and platelet responsiveness at the thrombotic site.

references.title: Biomarkers of in vivo platelet activation in coronary artery disease: a systematic review and meta-analysis: communication from the SSC of the ISTH.
references.journal: Journal of thrombosis and haemostasis : JTH

references.title: Glycoprotein V : the unsolved GPV puzzle.
A reliable plasma marker of platelet activation: does it exist?
GPV is a marker of in vivo platelet activation--study in a rat thrombosis model.
